title: Questions and statements about performance, internals, documenation, and other topics
body:
I am trying to use verilator on the .sv (or .v) output from Clash, the high level HDL. Clash has successfully generated  netlists and .sv files in Test/TestBench/ but when I run verilator .. -ITest/TestBench TestBench.sv it tells me it needs the types to be "predeclared" (what on earth does that mean!). OK, so I guess it wants the TestBench_type.sv module to be named on the command line first (please use explanatory and whole sentences in a manual, containing no jargon!) before TestBench.sv, so I do that and it generates files in obj_dir, but then hangs forever, seemingly:

% verilator -ITest/TestBench -Wno-fatal -Wno-lint --debug  --cc TestBench_types.sv  TestBench.sv

..
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:45: modSortByLevel()
- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/VTestBench_types_002_cells.tree
- V3LinkDot.h:37:     linkDotPrimary: 
- V3LinkJump.cpp:304: linkJump: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/VTestBench_types_007_link.tree
- V3LinkInc.cpp:251:  linkIncrements: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/VTestBench_types_008_linkInc.tree
- V3Param.cpp:855:    param: 
..[hums away forever without further output of any kind, no syscalls in strace] ...

ls -lrt obj_dir
...
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb     14442 Mar 31 12:34 VTestBench_types_TestBench_fpu4.vpp
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb    192510 Mar 31 12:34 VTestBench_types_TestBench_fpu.vpp
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb     18355 Mar 31 12:34 VTestBench_types_001_linkcells.dot
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb 433876616 Mar 31 12:34 VTestBench_types_002_cells.tree
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb 518593847 Mar 31 12:35 VTestBench_types_007_link.tree
-rw-r--r-- 1 ptb        97 Mar 31 12:35 VTestBench_types_008_linkInc.tree

and no more comes out, verilator sits there.

I have tried

verilator -ITest/TestBench -Wno-fatal -Wno-lint --debug -j --l2-name TestBench --bin TestBench --cc TestBench_types.sv --cc TestBench.sv

and the debug output still stops at the point indicated above. IMO it isgenerating TestBench_types stuff, where it should be generating TestBench stuff. HOW do I tell it to use the TestBench.sv file for an entry point, but take the type definitions used there from TestBench_types.sv?

I have tried making an executable with -exe and getting linking to a cc_main.cpp file that I have written working (which references TestBench, not TestBench_types), but it clearly never gets that far. I have tried --Build. I would just appreciate a clearly written explanation of what the flags I am giving even do! Why am I using -I, for example, when those are not (C/C++) "include" files in there but .sv files. What is the "includes" that the manual frustratingly says the flag is for, without defining what it means? Why can't it even find the TestBench.sv file unless I give -I/Test/TestBench ? That's not an "include"! The error message: "%Error: Cannot find file containing module: TestBench.sv" seems to say it is looking for a MODULE named TestBench.sv, whereas the manual says to put SOURCE FILES on the command line (" [source_files.v]"), so should I be specifying the MODULE instead of the SOURCE FILE?
Trying verilator -Wno-fatal -Wno-lint --debug -ITest/TestBench/ --cc TestBench seems to find the MODULE, in the SOURCE FILE, even thouh it says it is looking for INCLUDES, but then it compains about missing all the type definitions, which must be "predeclared"
%Error: Test/TestBench//TestBench.sv:9:14: Package/class 'TestBench_types' not found, and needs to be predeclared (IEEE 1800-2017 26.3)
Well! But if I have guessed what "predeclared" means (put TestBench_types earlier on the command line), it makes stuff called TestBench_types*, which surely lacks for anything to execute and it is no wonder nothing ever happens.

What is the right way to go about all this please? Is verilator just resting forever, or will it eventually produce something?
